name: San Juan de Plan a Camping El Forcullo by Sobrarbe Route 4 — circular Posets-Maladet Natural Park
name_original: San Juan de Plan a Camping El Forcullo por Sobrarbe Ruta 4 — circular Parque Natural Posets-Maladet
region_intro: Mountain biking around San Juan De Plan offers diverse terrain within the Aragonese Pyrenees, situated at an altitude of 1,120 meters. The region is characterized by towering peaks, extensive pine and fir forests, and high alpine meadows, providing a dramatic backdrop for challenging ascents. Many routes are located within or near the Posets-Maladeta Natural Park, featuring glacial lakes and the Cinqueta River valley. This landscape provides a wide range of mountain bike trails. Best mountain bike trails around San Juan De Plan The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierra de Chía Loop Trail, a 23.4 miles (37.6 km) difficult trail that takes about 4 hours to complete. This route features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is San Juan de Plan a Camping El Forcullo by Sobrarbe Route 4 — circular Posets-Maladet Natural Park, a difficult 19.9 miles (32.1 km) path. This trail offers panoramic views within the natural park. Local mountain bikers also love the Rabalsorri Trail, a 15.5 miles (25.0 km) trail leading through varied mountain landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 35 minutes. Mountain biking around San Juan De Plan is defined by Pyrenean peaks, dense forests, and glacial lakes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to very difficult routes. The routes in San Juan De Plan are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 2000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore San Juan De Plan's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around San Juan De Plan?
A: There are over 35 mountain bike trails available around San Juan De Plan, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. This includes 2 easy, 8 moderate, and 25 difficult routes.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, while many routes are challenging, there are a few easier options. The region offers 2 easy trails and 8 moderate trails that might be suitable for those with less experience or families looking for a more relaxed ride. These trails often traverse forests and meadows, providing a gentler introduction to mountain biking in the Pyrenees.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in San Juan De Plan?
A: The terrain around San Juan De Plan is highly varied, reflecting its location in the Aragonese Pyrenees. You can expect challenging ascents through towering peaks, extensive pine and fir forests, high alpine meadows, and routes that often feature glacial lakes and the Cinqueta River valley. Some trails may require pushing your bike due to their difficulty.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks I can see while mountain biking?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. Many trails are within or near the Posets-Maladeta Natural Park. You might encounter stunning glacial lakes, known as *ibones*, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/795895" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibón of Plan (Basa de la Mora)&lt;/a&gt;. Other highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3521429" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estós Valley&lt;/a&gt; and views of peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2503788" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Perdiguero Peak&lt;/a&gt;. The village itself also features traditional Pyrenean architecture.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are designed as circular trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210028476/san-juan-de-plan-a-camping-el-forcullo-by-sobrarbe-route-4-circular-posets-maladet-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan de Plan a Camping El Forcullo by Sobrarbe Route 4&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult circular trail offering panoramic views within the natural park. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977220242/sierra-de-chia-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Chía Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is also a challenging loop.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in San Juan De Plan?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 100 reviews. Reviewers often praise the stunning Pyrenean views, the diverse and challenging terrain, and the opportunity to explore the Posets-Maladeta Natural Park.

Q: Are there any facilities like cafes or pubs near the mountain bike trails in San Juan De Plan?
A: San Juan de Plan is a picturesque village with traditional Pyrenean architecture. While it offers a charming base, for a wider range of amenities like hotels, supermarkets, and restaurants, the nearby Pyrenean town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3480038" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castejón de Sos&lt;/a&gt; is a recognized hub for outdoor enthusiasts and a starting point for several mountain bike routes.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the mountain biking areas?
A: San Juan de Plan is nestled in the Aragonese Pyrenees. While specific public transport links directly to trailheads might be limited, the village itself can be accessed, and from there, many trails are within riding distance. It's advisable to check local bus schedules if you plan to rely on public transport for access to the wider region.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls?
A: While the immediate area around San Juan de Plan is known for its glacial lakes, the wider Pyrenean region features impressive waterfalls. A notable natural site, though a bit further afield, is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5186545" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac d'Oô&lt;/a&gt;, an artificial lake with an emblematic 275-meter waterfall, which could be an attraction for visitors to the wider region.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in San Juan De Plan?
A: The Pyrenean mountains around San Juan de Plan offer excellent conditions for mountain biking during the warmer months, typically from late spring through early autumn. During this period, the trails are generally clear of snow, and the weather is more favorable for exploring the high alpine meadows and challenging ascents. Winter can bring snow, making many trails inaccessible or requiring specialized equipment.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails?
A: Many natural areas in the Pyrenees, including parts of the Posets-Maladeta Natural Park, are generally dog-friendly, but it's crucial to keep dogs under control, especially due to the presence of wildlife and livestock. Always check specific trail regulations or park guidelines before heading out with your dog, as rules can vary.

Q: Are there any very difficult routes for experienced mountain bikers?
A: Yes, San Juan de Plan is known for its challenging terrain. Out of the 35 available routes, 25 are classified as difficult. Trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211592038/sobrarbe-chistau-valley-mtb-route-4" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sobrarbe: Chistau Valley MTB Route 4&lt;/a&gt;, covering over 47 km with significant elevation gain, are designed for very good fitness and advanced skills, often requiring riders to push their bikes on certain sections.
tips: Beautiful valley that is surrounded by peaks between 2,000 and 3,000 meters high, among which the Tuca Llardana (or Posets) stands out, which with 3,375 m. it is the second highest peak in the Pyrenees; In addition, it has abundant lakes, such as those of Millares, Barbarisa and Basa de la Mora (or Plan lake).
A small refuge at 1870 meters above sea level. It has a fireplace and space for a few people to escape a storm or spend the night.
